AMZN.bottom_line: Amazon stock is approaching its earnings report near all-time highs, up over 8% YTD -- the second-best performance among mega-cap tech behind only Broadcom. The rally has been driven by accelerating AWS growth expectations and the narrative that AI infrastructure spending is a multi-year tailwind for Amazon's cloud business. Investor sentiment is bullish but the bar is elevated.

bull_case.thesis_headline: Clean quarter + maintained guide re-rates AMZN.

pushes_higher.text: AWS growth trajectory: Can AWS accelerate from 24% to 30%+ in Q1? The AI workload ramp is the key driver, but competitive pressure from Azure and GCP is intensifying. Some analysts (Truist) see potential for 38% growth.

$200B capex plan: Amazon's aggressive AI infrastructure spend is the largest among hyperscalers. Bulls see it as securing competitive advantage; bears worry about return on investment timeline and potential overcapacity.
Union ruling impact: Recent labor rulings could increase costs in fulfillment operations. How material is the financial impact?

pushes_higher: $200B capex plan: Amazon's aggressive AI infrastructure spend is the largest among hyperscalers. Bulls see it as securing competitive advantage; bears worry about return on investment timeline and potential overcapacity.
Union ruling impact: Recent labor rulings could increase costs in fulfillment operations. How material is the financial impact?
pushes_lower: Union ruling impact: Recent labor rulings could increase costs in fulfillment operations. How material is the financial impact?
Tariff exposure: Amazon's first-party retail and third-party marketplace both have significant China-sourced inventory. Tariff escalation could pressure margins.

BKR.bottom_line: Baker Hughes enters Q1 2026 as one of the most direct beneficiaries of the Iran conflict and the resulting energy price surge. WTI crude spiked from ~$74 in late February to $115+ in early April before crashing back to ~$92-98 on ceasefire progress -- but Q1 average realized prices were significantly higher than Q4 2025. The oilfield services sector broadly benefits from elevated energy prices as E&P operators accelerate drilling programs and increase completions activity. BKR's Industrial & Energy Technology (IET) segment, which includes LNG equipment and gas technology services, is a structural growth engine as global LNG capacity expansion

bull_case.thesis_headline: Prior Quarter

pushes_higher.text: Iran conflict revenue upside: Did elevated commodity prices in Q1 drive incremental activity that flows through BKR's order book? International drilling activity in the Middle East (ex-Iran), offshore, and deepwater are the key vectors.

IET margins: The Industrial & Energy Technology segment has been expanding margins on the back of LNG equipment orders and service contract renewals. Can IET margins sustain 20%+ as the mix shifts toward higher-margin services?

Chart Industries acquisition integration: The proposed acquisition would add industrial gas, cryogenic equipment, and hydrogen capabilities. Watch for timing updates, regulatory approvals, and any deal-related charges.

base_case.setup_headline: In-line print, guidance maintained, narrative intact.

Oil price sensitivity: Q1 captured a broad range -- from ~$74 to $115+. BKR has less direct commodity exposure than E&P companies, but activity levels and customer sentiment are highly correlated with oil prices. The late-quarter WTI crash could moderate Q2 outlook commentary.

GOOG.bottom_line: Alphabet reports Q1 2026 after a mixed Q4 2025 (EPS $2.82 beat $2.62 by 7.6%, revenue solid). The stock has had a volatile 2026 as the market debates whether AI is a net positive or negative for Google's search franchise. Cloud growth has been strong but the core question is whether AI Overviews cannibalize search ad revenue. Consensus EPS: ~$2.63.

bull_case.thesis_headline: Clean quarter + maintained guide re-rates GOOG.

pushes_higher.text: Search disruption vs search enhancement: Is Google's AI integration (Gemini, AI Overviews) defensive or offensive? Bears argue AI reduces click-through to ads; bulls argue it increases engagement and query volume.
Cloud growth trajectory: Google Cloud has been the fastest-growing hyperscaler segment. Can it sustain 30%+ growth and path to profitability improvement?
YouTube monetization: YouTube Shorts monetization ramp and the TV/CTV advertising opportunity represent meaningful incremental revenue.

IBM.bottom_line: IBM enters Q1 2026 riding the strongest momentum in over a decade. FY2025 delivered $67.54B revenue (+7.6% YoY), $10.59B net income (+76% YoY), and $14.7B free cash flow -- a decade high. Management guided FY2026 to >5% constant-currency revenue growth and $15.7B FCF, above consensus expectations. The GenAI book of business stands at $12.5B cumulative (up from $9.5B QoQ), with software comprising >$2B and consulting >$10.5B. Q4 2025 was remarkable: revenue up 12% YoY (9% CC), software up 11%, infrastructure up 17% (Z17 mainframe cycle), and consulting grew modestly but with AI bookings accelerating. The stock has outperformed the S&P 500 by ~

bull_case.thesis_headline: Prior Quarter

pushes_higher.text: Software sustainability: Can software maintain 10%+ growth in Q1 after Q4's 11%? Red Hat guided mid-single-digit growth -- any deceleration below 5% would concern investors. Conversely, AI-driven demand for watsonx and automation could sustain double-digit software growth.

Consulting growth inflection: Consulting grew only ~3% in Q4. Management expects GenAI to be a consulting accelerator. Q1 will test whether the $3.6B GenAI ARR in consulting translates to revenue acceleration or whether macro headwinds are slowing enterprise spending.

Z17 mainframe cycle: Q4 infrastructure was up 17% on Z17 strength, outpacing the Z16 cycle. Q1 typically sees strong carryover from a new mainframe launch. Any deceleration below 10% would raise questions about cycle longevity.

INTC.bottom_line: Intel enters Q1 2026 at a critical inflection point. Q4 2025 delivered a surprise beat -- revenue $13.7B (beat $13.4B consensus), non-GAAP EPS $0.15 (beat $0.08) -- but stock fell 12% over two sessions on weak Q1 guidance: revenue $11.7-12.7B (midpoint below the $12.55B consensus) and non-GAAP EPS of $0.00 (break-even). Management cited supply constraints from the 18A process ramp and low available die supply as the binding constraint, with improvement expected in Q2 and beyond. The stock has recovered significantly since the January selloff, trading around $54 with a 42% YTD gain, partly fueled by renewed confidence in the x86 ecosystem's re

bull_case.thesis_headline: Prior Quarter

pushes_higher.text: Revenue recovery vs. supply constraint: Management guided $11.7-12.7B, with supply being "at its lowest level in Q1 before improving in Q2." If supply constraints ease faster than expected, there could be upside. If they persist, even guided revenue could be at risk. This is the single biggest varia

18A foundry pipeline: Intel Foundry Services (IFS) is the long-term strategic bet. Any updates on 18A customer wins, yield improvements, or foundry revenue pipeline would be catalysts. The Altera divestiture cleans up the P&L -- watch for any contribution from external foundry customers.

Client Computing Group trajectory: CCG was $7.6B in Q1 2025 (-8% YoY). PC replacement cycle dynamics and AI-PC adoption rates are key. Microsoft's Copilot+ push could drive a refresh cycle that benefits Intel's client silicon.

META.bottom_line: Meta reports Q1 2026 after a very strong Q2 2025 (EPS $7.28 beat $5.80 by 25.6%, Revenue $47.5B beat by 5.8%). The stock has been one of the strongest mega-cap performers on the back of AI-driven ad targeting improvements and Reels monetization gains. However, Reality Labs losses and elevated capex remain drags on sentiment. Consensus EPS: ~$6.59 for Q1. Last Q1 (2025): EPS $6.59, Revenue $42.3B.

bull_case.thesis_headline: Clean quarter + maintained guide re-rates META.

pushes_higher.text: AI ad targeting improvements: Meta's Advantage+ AI ad platform has been a game-changer for ad ROAS. Can the gains continue or are they plateauing?
Reality Labs losses: The Metaverse division continues to burn billions per quarter. When does the market stop tolerating these losses?
Capex intensity: 2026 capex guidance was $60-65B+, a massive increase driven by AI infrastructure. The market is watching for ROI signals.

UNH.bottom_line: UNH closed at $277.26 on April 2 (last trading day before Easter weekend). The stock is down ~16–18% YTD in 2026 and down ~47–50% over the past 12 months, marking one of the worst performances among large-cap healthcare names. The 52-week range spans approximately $234.60 – $606.36, reflecting the catastrophic multiple compression cycle that began in mid-2024 and accelerated through 2025 into 2026. At ~$277, the stock trades at roughly 15.5x forward FY2026 EPS consensus of $17.69 — well below its historical 20–25x range and near a decade-low valuation floor.

bull_case.thesis_headline: Clean quarter + maintained guide re-rates UNH.

pushes_higher.text: Medical Care Ratio (MCR) normalization — the single most important number. FY2026 guidance: 88.8% ±50bps, an improvement from 89.1% in 2025 (adjusted 88.9%). The Q1 print will be the first tangible data point on whether repricing is flowing through. Bulls argue repricing actions across commercial, M

Medicare Advantage 2027 rate environment — structural overhang. CMS's January 26 advance notice proposed only a 0.09% average rate increase for MA 2027 (vs. Street expectations of 4–6%, vs. 2026's 5.06% / $25B increase). The effective change per CMS's own math is ~2.54% once risk score trends are in

Optum Health restructuring — right-sizing vs. value destruction. Optum Health "strayed from the initial intent" (CEO Conway, Q3 2025 call) after a period of rapid physician group expansion. The division is now in active right-sizing: value-based care membership guided to decline ~10% in 2026 before

GS.bottom_line: Reinforces. The quarter validates the thesis that Goldman is the premier pure-play capital markets franchise. Record equities, 48% IB fee growth, and nearly 20% ROE show the operating leverage embedded in this business when the M&A cycle turns. The result reinforces a long bias in a Reflation regime that favors financials.

NFLX.bottom_line: Neutral. The underlying business is healthy -- 16% revenue growth, 32%+ margins, and a rapidly scaling ad business. But the EPS beat was quality-distorted by the WBD fee, and the 9-10% stock drop signals that the market was positioned for a guidance raise that didn't come. The stock remains a "premium growth" name that needs to demonstrate subscriber momentum without the subscriber disclosure.

STZ.bottom_line: For longs: The beer franchise is intact but not accelerating. Modelo and Corona remain category leaders, and the growth brands (Pacifico, Victoria) are delivering. FCF generation is strong ($1.8B), buybacks are aggressive, and the balance sheet is manageable at ~3x net leverage. The question is whether flat organic revenue growth and a 37-38% beer margin represent a new normal or a trough that improves as Veracruz costs are absorbed and tariff headwinds fade.

TSM.bottom_line: Strongly reinforces. TSMC is the clearest beneficiary of the AI infrastructure cycle. Four consecutive record quarters, 58% profit growth, 66%+ gross margins, and management guiding further acceleration -- this is the best fundamental setup in semis. The only risks are geopolitical (Taiwan/China) and customer concentration (NVDA/AAPL).
